profile.name: Arnab Mandal
profile.title: Electrical Engineering @ UofT | AI Researcher
profile.subTitle: Bridging the gap between powerful intelligence and physical engineering. I architect mixed-signal systems for low-power AI, integrating everything from analog circuitry and Verilog-based FPGA acceleration to machine learning deployment.
profile.heroDescription: Driven by a deep curiosity for how intelligence meets silicon. Exploring the physical limits of low-power AI through mixed-signal design and hardware acceleration.
profile.connectText: I'm always open to new conversations. Whether it's about mixed-signal design, a potential project, or just geeking out over tech, feel free to drop me a line.

education.degree: Bachelor of Applied Science in Electrical Engineering + PEY
education.details: Current GPA: 3.89/4.00 (87.6% average). NSERC Grant Holder. University of Toronto Book Award Recipient.

Awards: Student of the Year (2023), 2nd Place Academic Achievement Award. Activities: Tech Crew President, Student Council, Peer Tutoring, Jazz & Symphonic Band, Cross Country, Water Polo, and Debate.
TensorFlow and Deep Learning. Credential ID: DH52KL2GUERP
TensorFlow and Deep Learning. Credential ID: SLPN3KZ7RXUP

experience.company: Intelligent Sensory Microsystems Lab, University of Toronto
experience.description: Secured NSERC USRA grant ($7,500) for low-power hardware acceleration. Architecting hardware-software co-design for implantable hardware. Superheading hardware acceleration of core processing pipeline in Verilog on FPGA.
longDescription: Secured a nationally competitive NSERC USRA grant ($7,500) to fund research into low-power hardware acceleration.
Architecting a hardware-software co-design for low-power AI, strategically partitioning ML algorithms between on-chip implantable hardware and external processors.
Superheading the hardware acceleration of the core processing pipeline, implementing a closed-loop controller in Verilog on an FPGA to achieve sub-millisecond latency for real-time neuromodulation.
Led the complete migration of a 1100-line legacy research codebase from TensorFlow 1.15 to 2.15, resolving critical GPU incompatibilities.

experience.description: Researched Distributive Nonradiative Communication, involving complex mathematical proofreading and translating equations into heavily optimized Matlab models for simulation.
longDescription: Conducted deep literature reviews on Distributive Nonradiative Communication under the faculty of Engineering.
Translated high-level theoretical equations into scalable Matlab simulations to model communication fields.

experience.description: Applied advanced Statistical Regression models in R, writing code for LASSO, Ridge, and Elastic Net regressions to parse high-dimensional datasets.
longDescription: Focused entirely on high-dimensional Statistical Regression models.
Engineered reliable R code to implement LASSO, Ridge, and Elastic Net regressions on massive datasets to identify statistically significant parameters.

experience.description: Conducted high-volume COVID-19 screenings, ensuring hospital and staff safety through strict protocol enforcement and communication.
longDescription: Handled critical entrance screenings at WRHA facilities during the pandemic tail-end.
Deescalated tense situations and coordinated with nursing staff to maintain a secure perimeter.

experience.description: Developed a statistical regression model to predict cancer mortality rates across U.S. demographics under the supervision of Dr. Shakhawat Hossain.
longDescription: Assisted Dr. Shakhawat Hossain in statistical modeling to output highly accurate predictions.
Built models leveraging Python and R to predict state-by-state cancer mortality rates based on socioeconomic and genetic predictors.

experience.description: Architected LSTM, KNN, and SVM machine learning models completely from scratch to execute stock market predictions based on historical time-series data.
longDescription: Spearheaded early exploration into predictive Machine Learning models.
Wrote dedicated architectures for Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) neural networks, K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), and Support Vector Machines (SVM).

projects.title: Bio-Tensor MD: Molecular Dynamics ASIC
projects.description: A custom Application-Specific Integrated Circuit (ASIC) engineered exclusively for high-throughput Molecular Dynamics (MD), executing structural physics spatially.

projects.title: Software Defined Radio (SDR) Demodulator
projects.description: Designed a complete analog SSB Demodulator circuit with a multi-stage 100-4000 Hz active filter.

projects.title: FPGA-Based Quantum Circuit Simulator
projects.description: Engineered core quantum simulation firmware in C and Assembly for a Nios V processor.

projects.title: FPGA-Based Pacman Game in Verilog
projects.description: Architected a top-level module on a DE1-SoC FPGA with 640x480 60Hz VGA graphics pipeline.

projects.title: Autonomous Survival Rover
projects.description: Designed a two-brain robotics architecture utilizing an Arduino for real-time motor control and obstacle avoidance, paired with a cloud-based LLM for high-level strategic decision-making.

projects.title: Mobile Phone Monitoring Applications
projects.description: Collaborated with a team to design a conceptual application that repurposes existing smartphone sensors (like accelerometers) to monitor biometric data such as muscle fatigue during exercises, featuring a detailed feasibility study and signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) analysis.

courses.tier: Tier 1: 3rd & 4th Year Core Specialization (Hardware, Analog & Bio)
courses.description: Your most advanced, highly relevant coursework for ASIC/FPGA design and biomedical hardware.

courses.tier: Tier 2: 3rd & 4th Year Advanced Systems, Control & Economics
courses.description: The rest of your upper-year courses, covering high-level systems, networking, and engineering economics.

courses.tier: Tier 3: 2nd Year Foundational Hardware & Embedded Systems
courses.description: The critical second-year prerequisites that built your foundation in digital logic, computer architecture, and circuits.
Your introductory first-year engineering and mathematics courses.
